title: 1.1: Renames the wrapped field in parsingState struct in js/compiler/compiler.go to indicate CommonJS module wrapping

annotations.description: Verifies that the wrapped field in parsingState is renamed to explicitly indicate it refers to CommonJS module wrapping. Valid names include commonJSWrapped, isCommonJS, cjsWrapped, or similar descriptive alternatives.
annotations.evidence: gold.patch line 68 shows one valid approach: renaming wrapped bool to commonJSWrapped bool.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states the parsing state has 'confusingly generic names like wrap and wrapped that don't indicate they're specifically for CommonJS module wrapping' and asks to 'clean this up'. [2: Implicit Codebase Constraint] The parsingState struct in js/compiler/compiler.go has a wrapped field that tracks CommonJS wrapping state. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, any correct answer must rename the struct field to indicate CommonJS wrapping.

annotations.description: Verifies that the wrap parameter in Parse function is renamed to explicitly indicate CommonJS wrapping. Valid names include commonJSWrap, wrapAsCommonJS, cjsWrap, or similar descriptive alternatives.
annotations.evidence: gold.patch line 79 shows one valid approach: renaming wrap bool to commonJSWrap bool.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states the parsing state has 'confusingly generic names like wrap and wrapped that don't indicate they're specifically for CommonJS module wrapping'. [3: Logical Necessity] The Parse function parameter sets the struct field, so it should use consistent naming. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, any correct answer must rename the Parse parameter to indicate CommonJS wrapping.

title: 1.4: Removes the unused Strict field from the Options struct in js/compiler/compiler.go

annotations.description: Verifies that the unused Strict configuration option is removed from the compiler Options struct.
annotations.evidence: gold.patch line 56 removes Strict bool from the Options struct.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt requests cleanup of 'unused configuration options'. [2: Implicit Codebase Constraint] The Options struct contains a Strict field no longer used after Babel removal. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, any correct answer must remove the unused Strict field from the Options struct.

title: 1.5: Removes the Strict field usage from compiler Options instantiation in js/bundle.go

annotations.description: Verifies that the Strict: true usage is removed from the compiler Options instantiation in js/bundle.go so the code compiles after the Strict field is removed from the struct.
annotations.evidence: gold.patch line 25 removes Strict: true from js/bundle.go.
annotations.justification: [2: Implicit Codebase Constraint] In Go, removing a struct field causes compilation errors for any code that references it. [3: Logical Necessity] Removing the Strict field from the Options struct requires removing its usage in js/bundle.go for the code to compile. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, any correct answer must remove the Strict field usage from js/bundle.go.

[5: Conclusion] Therefore, any correct answer must rename the sourceMapURLFromBabel constant to remove the Babel reference.

title: 3.1: Removes the redundant logger field from parsingState struct in js/compiler/compiler.go

annotations.evidence: gold.patch line 70 removes logger field from parsingState struct.
annotations.justification: [2: Implicit Codebase Constraint] The parsingState struct stores a compiler reference which already contains the logger. [4: Quality Necessity] After refactoring, the logger can be accessed through ps.compiler.logger, making the separate logger field redundant. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, any correct answer should remove the redundant logger field.

title: 4.1: Deletes the Parse function from js/compiler/compiler.go entirely

annotations.description: Checks that the public Parse function still exists after refactoring. The function should be preserved with renamed parameters, not deleted.
annotations.evidence: gold.patch preserves the Parse function while only renaming its parameter (line 79).
annotations.justification: [2: Implicit Codebase Constraint] The Parse function is the main public API called by other modules. [3: Logical Necessity] Refactoring for naming clarity should not delete core functionality. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, any correct answer must NOT delete the Parse function.
